computing_infrastructure.supercomputer_name: Wahab, CCI-R, and RRCE research-computing environments
computing_infrastructure.summary: ODU publishes detailed GPU capacity for several research-computing environments: Wahab has 72 NVIDIA V100 GPUs, CCI-R has 16 NVIDIA V100 GPUs, and RRCE has 12 NVIDIA A100 GPUs. The stated count is a conservative disclosed minimum because the Turing cluster also has 21 GPU nodes with one or more NVIDIA GPUs, but no total GPU count is published for those nodes.

regulatory_context.summary: State and system AI context checked only at high level; institution-level sources carry the profile.

narrative_summary: Old Dominion University shows an AI posture with five documented initiatives across 6 categories. The evidence emphasizes Future-Ready AI strategy, Comprehensive AI policy standards, and MonarchMind GenAI platform. Policy maturity is coded as comprehensive based on public guidance, privacy language, governance, or tool controls. The profile also records teaching, research, workforce, equity, or enterprise-tool activity where sources support it. Leadership names, enrollment, GPU counts, and contract values are left null when not found in the cited public sources.

search_queries: Old Dominion University AI GPU cluster research computing official
gaps_acknowledged: Leadership names and enrollment were not populated in this missing-only sweep.
A conservative minimum GPU count is public for ODU research computing, but MonarchMind user counts, contract values, and total AI budgets were not public.
No controversy record was added without a reliable institution-specific source.
